stories.headline: Hiker discovers human remains in Douglas County, prompting evidence search
stories.summary:
A hiker discovered human remains in Douglas County on Wednesday, prompting an investigation by the Douglas County Sheriff's Office and the Douglas County Search and Rescue Team. Authorities searched the surrounding area for additional evidence following the discovery.

Details about the identity of the deceased or the circumstances surrounding the discovery have not been released. The investigation remains ongoing.

The discovery adds to a busy week for public safety agencies across the Denver metro area, which have been responding to multiple wildfires, high-wind events, and other emergencies.

stories.headline: Rol Race Motorsports operator launches fundraiser after Thornton fire destroys roughly 100 vehicles
stories.summary:
Jason Rol, operator of Rol Race Motorsports on West 84th Avenue in Thornton, has set up a GoFundMe fundraiser after Wednesday's grass fire burned approximately 100 vehicles belonging to him and his customers. Rol described the aftermath as "heartbreaking," noting that many of the cars consumed in the fire had been left in his care by customers of his auto restoration shop.

The fire ignited in a grassy area east of Pinnacle High School under red-flag conditions and spread rapidly due to dry conditions and high winds. Though no residential structures were lost, several businesses on the southern edge of the fire sustained damage.

Within 21 hours of the fundraiser's creation, community members had contributed more than $3,400 toward a $6,000 goal. Rol said he feels a deep responsibility to help those who trusted him with their vehicles and personal belongings.

stories.headline: Nuggets linked to Khris Middleton as Denver pursues buyout-market forward depth
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets have shown interest in former NBA champion Khris Middleton as part of an effort to add forward depth through the buyout market before the playoff-eligibility deadline on Sunday, March 1, according to Denver Post reporter Bennett Durando and NBA insider Marc Stein.

Middleton, currently with the Dallas Mavericks, told Stein he is "still weighing his options" regarding a potential buyout. The Mavericks have indicated they would work with him if he pursues a departure before the cutoff. Middleton won an NBA Championship with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2021 and holds career averages of 16.3 points, 4.7 rebounds, and 3.9 assists per game.

According to Durando, the Nuggets have been specifically targeting wing size and ball-handling in their buyout-market search. Kyle Anderson, another candidate Denver had shown interest in, signed with Minnesota earlier Thursday. The deadline to be playoff-eligible for a new team makes the decision time-sensitive for both Middleton and potential suitors.

stories.headline: Colorado Avalanche linked to Canucks defenseman Tyler Myers ahead of trade deadline
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche have been linked to Vancouver Canucks defenseman Tyler Myers ahead of the NHL trade deadline, according to Rick Dhaliwal of CHEKTV in Vancouver. The Avalanche completed a separate trade earlier this week, sending defenseman Sam Girard and a draft pick to the Pittsburgh Penguins in exchange for defenseman Brett Kulak.

Myers, an alternate captain in Vancouver, stands 6-foot-8 and weighs 229 pounds, which would make him the largest player on Colorado's roster. He has registered 65 hits and 91 blocked shots this season and is considered a reliable penalty killer.

With approximately $9 million in cap space available following the Girard trade, the Avalanche have been connected to several names including Ryan O'Reilly and Nazem Kadri. A potential Myers acquisition is viewed primarily as a depth move on the blue line as Colorado positions itself for a playoff run.

stories.headline: Mountain snow closes sections of I-70, creates heavy traffic through Colorado high country
stories.summary:
Snow and ice shut down several mountain highways in Colorado on Thursday morning, including a stretch of westbound Interstate 70 between Empire Junction and Georgetown, according to the Colorado Department of Transportation. The closure was lifted shortly before 9:30 a.m. as conditions improved.

CDOT cameras showed wet, snowy roads and backed-up traffic throughout Colorado's mountain corridors. Heavy traffic was reported in both directions of I-70 between Silverthorne and Golden for much of the morning.

The mountain snowfall contrasts with the persistently warm and dry conditions along the Front Range, where red-flag fire weather warnings remained in effect through Friday. Denver Water officials have noted the city's water supply is at historic lows due to the lack of winter precipitation.

stories.headline: Sierra Space appoints Dan Jablonsky as CEO after yearlong search
stories.summary:
Sierra Space, the Louisville-based commercial space company, has hired Dan Jablonsky as its new chief executive officer following a yearlong search for leadership. Jablonsky previously served as CEO of Maxar Technologies, which is headquartered in Westminster, and also has experience at Ursa Major.

Sierra Space is developing the Dream Chaser spaceplane and the LIFE habitat for orbital space stations, positioning itself as a key player in commercial space infrastructure. The company is a subsidiary of Sierra Nevada Corporation.

The hire brings a Colorado-rooted executive with deep experience in the space and geospatial intelligence industries to lead the company through a critical phase of development. Sierra Space has been working to secure contracts with NASA and other customers for its orbital systems.

stories.headline: Morphew daughters seek return of Suzanne Morphew's remains from investigators
stories.summary:
The adult daughters of Suzanne Morphew, who disappeared six years ago, are fighting to recover their mother's remains from investigators, according to the Colorado Springs Gazette. Barry Morphew, Suzanne's husband, has been charged with first-degree murder in her death.

Mallory and Macy Morphew have formally requested the return of their mother's remains, which are still in the custody of law enforcement. The case has drawn widespread attention in Colorado since Suzanne Morphew vanished from her Chaffee County home in May 2020.

The case involves complex legal proceedings as the murder charge against Barry Morphew moves through the courts. The daughters' request adds a new dimension to a case that has been closely watched across the state.

stories.headline: Denver area events for Friday, Feb. 27: Opera gala, Michelle Buteau, symphony and more
stories.summary:
Denver and the surrounding metro area host a variety of cultural events on Friday, Feb. 27, headlined by the Opera Colorado Grand Gala at the Denver Performing Arts Complex. The evening includes cocktails, dinner, a live auction, and performances, with tickets priced at $400.

Comedian Michelle Buteau performs at the Paramount Theatre at 7:30 p.m., with tickets starting at $57. The Lone Tree Symphony Orchestra presents "Winds of Destiny" at the Lone Tree Arts Center, and Cleo Parker Robinson Dance performs "We Have a Dream" at Parsons Theatre in Northglenn.

Other events include a Ken Walker Sextet jazz performance at Dazzle at Baur's, an electronic music show at Mission Ballroom featuring Drama and Amtrac, and the continuing run of "The Music Man" at the Buell Theatre.

topStory.headline: US and Israel launch coordinated strikes against Iranian nuclear and military sites
topStory.summary:
The United States and Israel launched a coordinated military operation against Iran early Saturday morning, striking nuclear enrichment facilities, missile production sites, and command infrastructure across multiple Iranian provinces. The strikes, which began around 2:00 AM local time, represent the largest direct military action against Iran by either nation and mark a dramatic escalation in the decades-long standoff over Iran's nuclear program.

President Trump authorized the operation following what the White House described as credible intelligence that Iran was within weeks of producing weapons-grade enriched uranium. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said in a televised address that the strikes targeted "the full spectrum of Iran's nuclear weapons infrastructure" and were conducted with "surgical precision" to minimize civilian casualties. Pentagon officials said the operation involved more than 200 aircraft sorties and cruise missile launches from naval vessels in the Persian Gulf and Arabian Sea.

Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei vowed retaliation, calling the strikes "an act of war that will not go unanswered." The Iranian military confirmed significant damage to facilities in Isfahan, Natanz, and Parchin, though it disputed US and Israeli claims about the extent of destruction to the nuclear program. The United Nations Security Council convened an emergency session within hours of the initial reports.

Global markets reacted sharply to the news, with oil prices surging more than 12 percent in early trading and stock futures plunging across major indices. Several airlines suspended flights across the Middle East, stranding thousands of travelers. European leaders called for immediate de-escalation, with French President Emmanuel Macron and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz issuing a joint statement urging all parties to return to diplomatic channels.

weather.forecast: It's gonna be 67 degrees today in Denver. That's a Friday temperature. The sky is gonna be overcast, which means the sun is up there, it's just not coming down. No rain, no snow, just clouds doing their thing, which I respect. Wind gets up to about 18 miles per hour, so your hat might leave, and honestly it might be happier somewhere else.

weekendEvents.title: 2026 Opera Colorado Grand Gala
weekendEvents.description: The Ellie Caulkins Opera House celebrates its twentieth anniversary with cocktails, dinner, and arias. The evening concludes at 8 p.m. with a concert featuring Grammy Award-winning baritone Will Liverman; tickets to the 8 p.m. concert are pay-what-you-wish.
weekendEvents.location: Denver Performing Arts Complex, 1400 Curtis Street, Denver

weekendEvents.title: We Have a Dream! Cleo Parker Robinson Dance
weekendEvents.description: Dancers of Cleo II, the second company of Cleo Parker Robinson Dance, present an uplifting performance celebrating the vision of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., incorporating his words and music. Tickets are $26–$35.
weekendEvents.location: Parsons Theatre at the Northglenn Arts & Cultural Center, 1 East Memorial Parkway, Northglenn

weekendEvents.title: Rick Glassman at Comedy Works Downtown
weekendEvents.description: Comedian Rick Glassman performs live at Comedy Works Downtown. Tickets are $25–$35.

weekendEvents.title: Arvada Winterfest
weekendEvents.description: Olde Town Arvada hosts this free annual festival celebrating different cultures through their winter traditions, featuring African drum demonstrations, mariachi performances, dragon and lion dances, Brazilian samba, and Irish dancing.
weekendEvents.location: Olde Town Arvada Square, 5726 Olde Wadsworth Blvd., Arvada

weekendEvents.title: Denver Beer Co. Coffee, Beer & Bacon Festival
weekendEvents.description: Denver Beer Co.'s five locations host this annual event featuring unlimited samples of coffee from Huckleberry Roasters, craft beer, and breakfast food including Voodoo Doughnuts. Tickets are $33–$65.
weekendEvents.location: Denver Beer Co. (all five locations), Denver

weekendEvents.title: Après at The Terminal: Patio Sessions at Union Station
weekendEvents.description: Terminal Bar at Union Station hosts a free alpine après-ski party with complimentary cocktails for guests 21+, an ice luge, winter music, photo ops, and giveaways including a snowboard and camping stove. This is a 21+ event.
weekendEvents.location: Terminal Bar at Union Station, Denver

weekendEvents.title: Cosmic Winterwonderfest
weekendEvents.description: Head to the mountain town of Jamestown for a day of live music, psychic readings, food, and beverages at the Jamestown Mercantile. Tickets are $20.

weekendEvents.title: Christopher Titus at Comedy Works South
weekendEvents.description: Stand-up comedian Christopher Titus performs two shows at Comedy Works South in Greenwood Village. Tickets are $32–$37.
weekendEvents.location: Comedy Works South, 5345 Landmark Place, Greenwood Village

weekendEvents.title: Brick Planet: A Magical Journey Made with LEGO Bricks
weekendEvents.description: This ongoing exhibit at the Denver Museum of Nature & Science features large-scale LEGO brick creations and is open all weekend. Admission is free for members and children ages 2 and under; $20.95–$25.95 for general admission.
weekendEvents.location: Denver Museum of Nature & Science, 2001 Colorado Blvd., Denver

weekendEvents.title: Dairy Block Petite Parade Mardi Gras Celebration
weekendEvents.description: The Dairy Block hosts its eighth annual mini Mardi Gras celebration, featuring a tiny float parade, live music, dancing, and food. Attendees are invited to build a creative shoebox float on wheels; registration is free and required.

weekendEvents.title: Four Mile Historic Park Free Admission Day
weekendEvents.description: Thanks to the Scientific and Cultural Facilities District (SCFD), Four Mile Historic Park offers free admission today as part of its rotating free-day program.

weekendEvents.title: Cupid's Undie Run
weekendEvents.description: Participants run a mile in their underwear to raise funds for those affected by neurofibromatosis (NF), a group of genetic conditions causing tumors to grow on nerves. The event includes pre-run drinking and dancing and ends with a dance party.
weekendEvents.location: Stoney's Bar & Grill, 1111 Lincoln Street, Denver
